narrative.what_this_demonstrates: An internal tool that is mostly correctly sized. Compute shows slightly low utilization (below the ideal but not below the threshold that justifies aggressive action). All other tiers are in healthy ranges. The right action is a minor compute adjustment, no other changes.
narrative.why_this_scenario_exists: Tests the border case between fully healthy (no action) and partial-optimization (targeted action). The signal is on the edge — a more aggressive agent over-recommends; a less aggressive one misses the small improvement.
narrative.what_the_agent_should_conclude: Reduce compute fleet from 5 to 4 t3.medium instances. No other changes. Expected savings ~$110/month.
narrative.what_distinguishes_good_from_great: A good recommendation reduces compute count by 1. A great recommendation explicitly notes that the system is "mostly well-optimized" and that only a minor adjustment is warranted — framing the recommendation as refinement rather than fix.

before_after_evidence.observed_outcome_summary: Compute utilization lifted modestly on the remaining instances, application_p95_latency_ms held in the 130–200ms band, SLA 99.5% preserved, monthly cost dropped by approximately $110.
before_after_evidence.source_attribution: Pattern observed across internal-tool cohort during Q4 2025.

target_recommendation.specific_change: Reduce compute fleet from 5 to 4 t3.medium instances. Do not change instance class. Leave database and network unchanged.
